steps.title: Use the one-step ratio
steps.content: For $f(x)=a\cdot b^{x}$, moving from $x=n-1$ to $x=n$ multiplies the output by $b$. In other words, $f(n)=b\cdot f(n-1)$.

steps.title: Rewrite the given equation as a percent left over
steps.content:
The equation says
$$f(n-1)=f(n)+\frac{82}{100}f(n-1)$$
Subtract the $82\%$ part from both sides:
$$f(n)=\left(1-\frac{82}{100}\right)f(n-1)=\frac{18}{100}f(n-1)$$

steps.title: Match that ratio to b
steps.content:
Because $f(n)=b\cdot f(n-1)$, the multiplier from $n-1$ to $n$ is exactly $b$. Therefore
$$b=\frac{18}{100}=\frac{9}{50}=0.18$$
A quick Desmos check is just evaluating the percent left after subtracting $82\%$. The answer is 0.18 or 9/50.
